invented_voltage_level.evidence: All voltages cited (+12-24 VDC for inputs, +5-24 VDC for outputs, 20-28 VDC supply) match text printed on the page verbatim: 'DC inputs typically operate in the range of +12-24 VDC' and the labeled '20-28 VDC' battery symbol in the output diagram.

invented_device_tag.evidence: Device names used (DC NPN Sensor (Sinking), DC PNP Sensor (Sourcing), PLC DC Input, PLC DC Output, Supply, 20-28 VDC) all correspond to labels actually printed on the drawing; no fabricated tags/part numbers were introduced, and the response explicitly notes 'No device tags, wire numbers, terminal numbers... are printed.'

incorrect_terminal_or_destination_as_fac.evidence: The 'map' response states with 0.85 confidence: '20-28 VDC loop power for the output example — 20-28 VDC @ Power / +DC Power → PLC DC Output and field-device load loop.' In the drawing, the '+DC Power' wire feeds the separate 'Power' terminal of the PLC output block (a distinct internal supply path), while the 20-28 VDC battery symbol sits in the Common-to-Ground return path (the same pattern as the 'Supply' battery in the NPN input example, which sits between Common and Ground, not at the Input/Output signal path). Conflating these two distinct power nodes as the same node and stating it as a confident fact is a terminal/connection error not supported by the drawing's visible topology.

missed_hazardous_voltage_clearly_shown.evidence: The drawing shows only low-voltage DC (max 20-28 VDC); no AC or hazardous voltage is depicted, and the response correctly notes 'no higher/AC levels are shown.'

false_certainty_on_illegible_area.evidence: No illegible regions were misrepresented as certain; the response appropriately flags the absence of tags/part numbers as 'Couldn't confirm.'

non_electrical_image_treated_as_print.evidence: The image is genuinely an electrical wiring reference page (CLICK PLC manual Ch.3, p.3-31) and was correctly treated as such.

sheet_identity.note: Correctly identifies 'Manual page 3-31 (CLICK PLC Hardware User Manual, Ch. 3)' matching the visible header 'Chapter 3: Installation and Wiring' and page number '3–31'.

circuit_purpose.note: Accurately summarizes the page as teaching sinking/sourcing DC input and output wiring methods, matching the visible section headers 'DC Input Wiring Methods' and 'DC Output Wiring Methods'.

device_identification.note: Devices (opto-coupler input, NPN/PNP sensors, PLC DC Output, field device load, supplies) match the boxes and labels visible in the drawing (e.g., 'DC NPN Sensor (Sinking)', 'DC PNP Sensor (Sourcing)').

wire_cable_identification.note: Most conductor pairings (e.g., 'NPN sensor output -> PLC sourcing input') match the drawing, but 'Supply(+) -> load Input (sourcing)' misroutes the 20-28 VDC supply into the signal path instead of the Common-Ground return path shown in the drawing.

terminal_mapping.note: The claim '20-28 VDC ... @ Power / +DC Power → PLC DC Output and field-device load loop' incorrectly merges the separate 'Power'/'+DC Power' terminal with the 20-28 VDC supply node, which the drawing places in the Common-to-Ground path (per the same layout pattern used for 'Supply' in the NPN example).

voltage_identification.note: Correctly lists '+12-24 VDC', '+5-24 VDC', and '20-28 VDC' exactly as printed near the respective diagrams.

cross_reference_accuracy.note: Confidence scores (0.80-0.85) are applied uniformly to both correct and incorrect claims (e.g., the erroneous Power/20-28VDC linkage carries the same 0.85 confidence as verified NPN/PNP mappings), showing poor internal cross-checking.

uncertainty_calibration.note: The ambiguous Power-terminal-vs-supply relationship in the output diagram was asserted at 0.85 confidence rather than flagged as uncertain, despite it being the least visually explicit connection on the page.

unsupported_or_invented_claims.note: "20-28 VDC loop power for the output example — 20-28 VDC @ Power / +DC Power → PLC DC Output and field-device load loop" asserts a node relationship not clearly supported by the drawing's wiring pattern.

technician_usefulness.note: Generally useful generic wiring explanation, but the incorrect Power/20-28VDC terminal linkage could mislead a technician tracing the output loop's actual power source.

safety_language.note: Appropriately generic and correct: 'de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first' for continuity checks, matching the low-voltage DC content actually shown.

readability.note: Well-organized with clear headers (Signals, Devices, Wires/cables) and consistent formatting throughout both responses.

response_completeness.note: Covers all three diagrams on the page (NPN input, PNP input, DC output) and offers deeper 'map' detail as requested.

map_offer_supported.note: The map follow-up does deliver terminal/wire detail as promised, but includes the erroneous Power/+DC Power–to–20-28VDC linkage, undermining the value of the delivered detail.

verified_strengths: Correctly identifies the page/section as CLICK PLC manual Ch.3 p.3-31 DC Input/Output Wiring Methods.
Correctly captures the NPN sinking sensor -> PLC sourcing input wiring with external Supply in the Common/Ground return path, matching the diagram.
Correctly captures the PNP sourcing sensor -> PLC sinking input wiring with no additional supply needed, matching the diagram text 'no additional power supply is required for the module.'
Correctly cites all printed voltage ranges (+12-24 VDC, +5-24 VDC, 20-28 VDC) without inventing new values.
Appropriate generic safety/LOTO language for a low-voltage DC training diagram.

suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.claim: 20-28 VDC loop power for the output example — 20-28 VDC @ Power / +DC Power → PLC DC Output and field-device load loop
suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.why: The drawing shows '+DC Power' feeding a separate 'Power' terminal on the PLC DC Output block, while the 20-28 VDC battery symbol sits in the Common-to-Ground return path (consistent with the 'Supply' battery placement in the NPN input example above it); conflating these two nodes is not supported by the visible layout.

Per the drawing's wiring pattern, the direct signal path is PLC 'Output (sinking)' to Field Device 'Input (sourcing)'; the supply is inserted in the Common-Ground return path, not directly wired to the load's Input terminal.

items_requiring_technician_review: Verify actual node connections of the 'Power'/'+DC Power' terminal versus the 20-28 VDC supply in the PLC DC Output diagram before relying on the assistant's stated loop topology.
Confirm whether '+DC Power' and the 20-28 VDC supply are electrically the same net or two independent supplies before troubleshooting an output circuit based on this page.
summary: The response correctly identifies the sheet, purpose, and most sinking/sourcing input wiring paths, and cites voltages accurately from the page. However, the 'map' follow-up confidently (0.85) mis-states the output-circuit topology by merging the '+DC Power/Power' terminal with the separate 20-28 VDC supply node shown in the Common-to-Ground return path, an incorrect terminal/connection claim presented as fact, which caps the grade at F despite otherwise solid generic descriptions.
